gold_answer: Netflix's 2026 10-K excerpt does not name any specific shareholder activist, bidder, or hostile-takeover attempt. Instead, it discloses three generic charter provisions that could discourage takeovers: (1) authorization for the board to issue up to 10,000,000 shares of undesignated preferred stock without stockholder approval, (2) a prohibition on stockholder action by written consent, and (3) advance notice requirements for proposing matters at stockholder meetings. It also notes that Netflix is subject to Delaware anti-takeover provisions and that the company has announced plans to modify some of these provisions over time.
